summary: Implemented M6 Alpha client surfaces through client read-model slices and protocol command submission: scenario selection, start-to-victory command flow, diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, policy/event, encyclopedia, AI/adviser reasons, map candidate display, local checkpoint save/load, and victory/failure status. React owns only DOM/session progress; map rendering remains inside map-renderer; no sim-core, desktop, telemetry, server/account, arbitrary-code mod, or manual node battle implementation was added.

tests_run.result: Modified paths are limited to allowed client/test/task/message paths for M6-CLIENT-ALPHA-SURFACES-001.
1 test file passed; 17 tests passed, including M6 Alpha read-model and checkpoint parser coverage.
1 test file passed; 7 tests passed, including M6 Alpha non-placeholder major-system SSR coverage.
1 test file passed; 4 tests passed, including existing M6 map candidate renderer read-model coverage.
Vite web build passed; existing chunk-size warning reported for large bundles.
Storybook build completed successfully; existing chunk-size warning reported.

10 Chromium Playwright tests passed, including M6 Alpha scenario-to-victory flow, checkpoint save/load, diplomacy/legitimacy/succession, policy/event, encyclopedia, map candidate display, no forbidden UI entry buttons, and required viewport overflow checks.
Architecture boundaries, sim determinism, full Vitest suite, and web package tests passed; 39 root test files / 301 tests passed plus web 1 file / 4 tests.
Prettier format check, ESLint, recursive package typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.
Task JSON validated after status transition to REVIEW.

decisions: Added M6 Alpha as a client read-model slice in client-core instead of storing full WorldState or reimplementing sim-core authority in React.
Used protocol M6 Alpha script and authoritative command DTOs for UI command submission; UI only tracks local session phase, preview, confirmed command ids, and checkpoint text.
Kept map candidate display as read-model metadata plus existing map-renderer-owned rendering path; React does not own per-entity Pixi DisplayObjects.
Used Playwright rather than editing .storybook because the task allowed paths did not include .storybook/**.
Fixed duplicate reason-chip React keys with index-qualified keys, resolving the previously observed client warning without changing reason-code semantics.
risks: M6 surfaces are still fixture/read-model projections over accepted protocol scripts and existing read models; wiring to a live worker query stream remains future integration work.
Storybook build passes, but M6 normal/empty/error/extreme state coverage is represented by Playwright and web fixture modes rather than new .storybook stories due allowed-path limits.
Web build and Storybook build still emit existing large chunk warnings; no new dependency or bundle split decision was introduced.
requested_action: Independently review M6-CLIENT-ALPHA-SURFACES-001 for acceptance criteria, architecture boundaries, read-model/command discipline, viewport coverage, forbidden-surface exclusions, and test sufficiency. Return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK; do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED.
